acceptance_targets: [CH18_OSEN_CONVENIENCE_PAUSE] In revised material around snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:143-159, Osen must either take a concrete disabling or containment action against Kem or Sable, or the narration must state a specific physical or threshold constraint that makes such action impossible or self-defeating. If she still only issues a warning while an unopposed third contributor arrives, fail.

[CH18_THRESHOLD_MECHANICS_OPACITY] Across revised material at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:115-123 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:141-157, a cold reader must be able to say what Vel's first two threshold spends do, why Kem's remaining two levels cannot finish the crossing, and how Sable's seven levels alter the failing tear without sending Sable through it. If any of those links still requires outside-chapter inference, fail.

[CH18_FRAGMENT_COMPRESSION_TIC] In revised narration at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:9, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:23, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:27, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:47, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:57, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:77, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:95, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:101, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:127, the action may stay compressed, but the beats should read as complete clauses or clearly motivated free-indirect thought. If multiple isolated fragment stubs remain the default scaffold in those cited beats, fail.

[CH18_CROSSING_UNDEREMBODIED] The crossing-to-zero passage renders at least two distinct physical sensations of the Ledger's absence (not just 'silence'). Vel's loss of Perception is shown through a concrete change in how she experiences her immediate environment. The passage length and rendering density are at least equal to Kem's Ledger-absence passage in Ch19.
[LW08-003] Turning from chapter 17 to chapter 18 produces immediate forward movement with a materially changed tactical state, not another version of the same pinned-behind-rock stalemate.

[LW09-001] A first-time reader can move from the last page of chapter 17 to the first page of chapter 18 without pausing to recalculate elapsed time, lost ground, or where Vel and Kem picked up their new injuries.
[length_chapter_18_min_words] Chapter word count is >= 6500 while preserving heading contract.

findings.acceptance_test: In revised material around snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:143-159, Osen must either take a concrete disabling or containment action against Kem or Sable, or the narration must state a specific physical or threshold constraint that makes such action impossible or self-defeating. If she still only issues a warning while an unopposed third contributor arrives, fail.
findings.problem: Osen's inaction at the failing tear reads as author convenience. After clocking the descending figure on the scree and spending the whole chapter preventing crossings, she reaches Kem at the edge of the opening and does nothing but tell him to stay put while the rescue arrives. The chapter needs a concrete tactical or threshold constraint here, or an attempted intervention, to keep the turn from feeling like a held-open lane for Sable.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the confrontation at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:143, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:145, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:149, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:151, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:159 so Osen's choice is constrained rather than convenient. Options include giving her a specific threshold-risk that prevents contact, showing her attempt a restraint that Kem or Sable interrupts, or making clear why acting would worsen the outcome she is trying to prevent.

findings.acceptance_test: Across revised material at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:115-123 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:141-157, a cold reader must be able to say what Vel's first two threshold spends do, why Kem's remaining two levels cannot finish the crossing, and how Sable's seven levels alter the failing tear without sending Sable through it. If any of those links still requires outside-chapter inference, fail.
findings.problem: The cascade failure and stabilization mechanics are only partly legible on the page. Vel spends into the ground, then to hold the opening, then to zero; the tear fails because two people had not been enough; Sable's seven levels can somehow steady and widen it from above zero. The observable sequence is clear, but the causal chain is too compressed at the exact point where the chapter asks the reader to understand why this climax works.
findings.rewrite_direction: Clarify the operational chain at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:115, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:119, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:121, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:141, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:151, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:157. One or two precise sentences from Vel's understanding can define what each spend is doing, why Kem's remaining two levels cannot complete the crossing, and why Sable's contribution stabilizes the tear instead of crossing her.

findings.acceptance_test: In revised narration at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:9, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:23, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:27, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:47, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:57, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:77, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:95, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:101, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:127, the action may stay compressed, but the beats should read as complete clauses or clearly motivated free-indirect thought. If multiple isolated fragment stubs remain the default scaffold in those cited beats, fail.
findings.problem: The narration leans repeatedly on clipped fragment stubs and tally bursts: Too regular for wind. Ugly move. Late by a breath. Alive. Five left. Four left. Five. Three left. Zero. In isolation these can sharpen pressure; at this density they become a mechanical chapter-level scaffold, reading like telegraphic compression rather than controlled close-third prose.
findings.rewrite_direction: Replace the recurrent clipped-stub scaffolding at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:9, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:23, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:27, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:47, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:57, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:77, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:95, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:101, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:127 with grammatically complete compressed sentences or cleaner free-indirect phrasing. Preserve arithmetic pressure without relying on telegraphic narration as the default action rhythm.

findings.acceptance_test: The crossing-to-zero passage renders at least two distinct physical sensations of the Ledger's absence (not just 'silence'). Vel's loss of Perception is shown through a concrete change in how she experiences her immediate environment. The passage length and rendering density are at least equal to Kem's Ledger-absence passage in Ch19.
findings.evidence: Lines 4317–4343. Vel's transition from her final levels to zero occupies approximately 15 lines. The key sensory moment is dispatched with 'Silence hit.' Compare lines 4400–4408; where Kem's experience of the Ledger's absence in Ch19 receives a full paragraph of embodied rendering: the background hum; its disappearance; what the body feels without it.; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:123; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:127; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:129; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:133; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:137
findings.problem: Vel has lived with the Ledger for 58 years. The novel has carefully tracked every level she spends — each one noted, felt, and paid for. The moment the count reaches zero is the most consequential sensory event in the book: forty years of background hum vanishing, the Perception that has defined her relationship to the world going silent, the body adjusting to a fundamentally different state of being. This moment gets less rendering than Kem's secondary experience of the same absence one chapter later. The asymmetry is not purposeful restraint — it is underwriting at the novel's climactic moment.
findings.rewrite_direction: Expand the zero-crossing passage. Render the physical sensation of the Ledger vanishing — what Vel's body does when the count reaches zero. Show the absence of Perception specifically: she has used it as her primary sense for decades, and losing it should be like losing hearing or proprioception. The cascade is still happening around her; show what the world looks like without the Ledger's overlay. Include at least two distinct physical sensations of the Ledger's absence beyond silence, a Vel-specific recognition or emotional jolt, and one concrete bodily aftereffect before or during the crossing. Match or exceed the rendering density that Kem receives in Ch19 for his Ledger-absence experience.

findings.acceptance_test: Turning from chapter 17 to chapter 18 produces immediate forward movement with a materially changed tactical state, not another version of the same pinned-behind-rock stalemate.
findings.evidence: Chapter 17 ends with Vel and Kem sprinting from the tunnel to a small rock shelf while Osen calls, "You can't make it work. It's not enough anymore" at snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4190 and snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4200. Chapter 18 then opens after an off-page three-hour summary with them again pinned behind inadequate cover, still at twelve and eight, under the same strategic condition at snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4205 and snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4209.

evidence.excerpt: 4200: "You can't make it work," she called. "It's not enough anymore."
4205: Nearly three hours after the commander told them the threshold might fail, Vel and Kem had two hundred meters left and nowhere decent to hide.

4209: She crouched behind a rib of sandstone that covered half a shoulder if a person believed in miracles. Her left ankle hurt from a bad landing. Her mouth tasted like old dust. Kem lay three meters off her left knee, breathing through his mouth, chin already bloodied from one earlier fall, still at twelve. She still had eight. Four Reserve agents held the basin. Two garrison fighters ahead. The blink-step specialist circling south. The commander at the point itself, patient as drought.

findings.problem: The boundary stages the same beat twice: exposed run, insufficient cover, commander warning, no meaningful level change. That repetition blunts the cliff and makes the climax feel as if it restarts instead of accelerates.
findings.rewrite_direction: Either end chapter 17 earlier, before the first scramble to cover, or open chapter 18 on the first decisive change after that moment: a spend, a casualty, a broken line, or the threshold point suddenly becoming reachable.

findings.acceptance_test: A first-time reader can move from the last page of chapter 17 to the first page of chapter 18 without pausing to recalculate elapsed time, lost ground, or where Vel and Kem picked up their new injuries.
findings.evidence: Chapter 17 ends with the first exposed sprint from the rock shelf and Osen's immediate warning (snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4194, snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4200), but chapter 18 opens after an already-fought attritional phase, with only two hundred meters left and new injuries summarized after the fact (snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4205, snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4209).

evidence.excerpt: 4194: It was not much of a run. Eight and twelve did not produce speed so much as refusal. Kem's boots slipped on polished stone. Heat struck up through his soles. The alarm bounced off the basin walls. By the time they hit the shelf of rock, the two descent agents were already appearing at the base of the switchback and fanning wide.
4200: "You can't make it work," she called. "It's not enough anymore."
4205: Nearly three hours after the commander told them the threshold might fail, Vel and Kem had two hundred meters left and nowhere decent to hide.

4209: She crouched behind a rib of sandstone that covered half a shoulder if a person believed in miracles. Her left ankle hurt from a bad landing. Her mouth tasted like old dust. Kem lay three meters off her left knee, breathing through his mouth, chin already bloodied from one earlier fall, still at twelve. She still had eight. Four Reserve agents held the basin. Two garrison fighters ahead. The blink-step specialist circling south. The commander at the point itself, patient as drought.

findings.problem: The break lands in active combat and then advances past a large piece of that same combat, so the reader has to reconstruct what happened off-page instead of feeling the escalation continuously. The opening of chapter 18 reads less like a clean handoff and more like missing steps in the basin fight.
findings.rewrite_direction: Either end chapter 17 before the first sprint commits the scene to immediate action, or let chapter 18 reopen on the shelf under fire and dramatize the attritional sideways push before compressing later. The boundary needs one continuous action line from alarm to 'two hundred meters left.'

findings.acceptance_test: Chapter word count is >= 6500 while preserving heading contract.
findings.rewrite_direction: Add causally necessary scene material that strengthens the chapter engine, increases pressure, and lands the planned state shift without filler.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

Every narration sentence must be grammatically complete. Do not use article-stripped noun-phrase fragments, subjectless stubs, or telegraphic fragment strings as a rhythmic device. Isolated level-count callouts ('Twelve.' or 'Zero.') are acceptable; stacked fragment clusters ('Not night. Underground dark.' or 'Too slow. Too loud. Too late.') are not. Achieve compression through short complete sentences, not by dropping subjects and articles.

When a scene beat has landed through concrete image, action, dialogue, or bodily sensation, do not add an abstract summary sentence that restates its meaning in thesis language. If a paragraph's only job is to translate what the reader already understood from the prior image or line of dialogue, cut it or replace it with a new fact, consequence, or pressure. Trust the scene. One beat, one landing.

No character should deliver a multi-sentence thematic diagnosis, balanced mini-speech, or quotable aphorism during a pressured scene. Accusations, confessions, and revelations must arrive through messy, character-true speech: interruptions, self-corrections, false starts, incomplete grammar, practical redirects, and mid-sentence recalibrations. If a line of dialogue can be lifted as a standalone epigram, it is probably too composed for the scene pressure. Rhetorical questions used for emphasis rather than genuine inquiry are not permitted for Vel.

revision_pass.focus: Prioritize structural and craft blockers first: causality, chapter architecture, stakes clarity, continuity logic, and all HIGH/CRITICAL defects.
revision_pass.label: HIGH/CRITICAL structural/craft fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This is a dark comedy about a world where doing nothing is rational and doing something is self-destruction. Both the comedy and the darkness must be fully committed. Do not soften combat into choreography or spectacle — fights should hurt, cost real things, and leave damage. Do not soften the comedy into gentle satire — the absurdity of the Hoarded and their agents should be genuinely funny, sometimes cruelly so. Do not soften grief into sentimentality or revelation into epiphany. The premise demands a tonal register that holds absurdity and genuine pain in the same hand. When the material is uncomfortable — the physical reality of injury, the moral weight of exploitation, the specific grief of losing someone to a place you cannot reach — lean into specificity rather than retreating to safety. A draft that never risks anything produces a novel that never moves anyone.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism in combat, injury, consequence, or emotional extremity is a craft failure. If Vel breaks ribs, the reader should feel the break — the specific wrongness of breathing with cracked bone, the way she holds herself, the pain when she forgets. If Kem is terrified, the terror should be specific and physical: hands, stomach, breathing, the precise quality of his fear. Do not abstract away from embodied experience into summary or generalization.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the norm for all characters except Sable (low) and early-book Kem (initially low, shifting to high). Uncontracted forms are reserved for deliberate emphasis, formal Hoarded speech contexts, or bureaucratic register. In casual conversation, unjustified full-form lines should be treated as suspect.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Vary chapter rhythm aggressively. Combat chapters should be compressed and physical. Discovery chapters should have more room for observation and the surprise of new information. Quiet chapters should breathe — longer scenes, more sensory detail, conversations that circle rather than drive. Intercut chapters (like Ch 11's Kem/Sable split and Ch 17's Sable intercut) should have contrasting textures between sections. No two consecutive chapters should have the same structural shape: vary scene count, dialogue density, pacing, and the ratio of action to interiority. Dark comedy chapters in the back half (Ch 12, 14, 15, 17) must commit fully to comic energy as a primary narrative mode: the absurdity of Hoarded bureaucratic language applied to human exploitation, the grim humor of fighting for your life with single-digit levels, the comedy of precision teamwork performed in emotional silence. These chapters carry genuine stakes alongside their comedy — the tonal fusion is the point, not tonal alternation.
prose_style_profile.diction: Concrete, physical, occasionally wry. Avoids both literary pretension and genre cliche. The world has its own vocabulary (Hoarded, Spent, Ledger, Stillness, the Scour, the Margins) and these terms should be used naturally, not defined in exposition. Metaphors earned from the world's own materials — spending, counting, weight, erosion — rather than imported from outside the setting. The Hoarded institutional register — bureaucratic classifications, procedural euphemism, administrative language applied to human exploitation — is a specific comic instrument in the Margins chapters. When rendering Hoarded documents, policies, or institutional speech, the language should be precise, procedural, and devastating in its indifference.
forbidden_drift_patterns: Narrator-as-comedian: comedy arises from situation and character, not from narrative commentary or winking asides to the reader
Ability-use described like video game tooltips: do not produce stat readouts or system notifications in prose
Emotional temperature permanently at maximum: not every scene is a crisis and not every internal moment is an epiphany
Generic fantasy description: landscape, architecture, and weather must be specific to this world's conditions, not recycled from the fantasy-prose commons
Competence porn: do not let characters be consistently impressive — they should fumble, miscalculate, and fail at things that are hard
Thematic underlining: the prose should not highlight its own meanings or signal when something is symbolically significant
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third-person limited, shifting with the POV character. Vel chapters are compressed and physical — the prose should feel like it is counting. Kem chapters are more expansive and curious — the prose has more room for observation and surprise. Sable chapters are structured and institutional, loosening as she changes. The POV break in Ch 13–14 (two consecutive Kem chapters) should make Vel's absence felt: the reader is locked inside Kem's betrayed perspective, and the prose should reflect the claustrophobia of a mind cycling through anger without access to the other person's interiority. The distance should be intimate enough to access characters' internal arithmetic but not so close it becomes stream-of-consciousness.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Varied by chapter texture and scene pressure. Short declarative sentences in combat. Longer, conditional, clause-heavy sentences in reflection. Comma-heavy run-ons in panic or exhilaration. The Ledger's arithmetic should bleed into prose rhythm during ability-use moments — characters counting, weighing, calculating. Quiet chapters should breathe. Action chapters should compress. Two consecutive chapters should not share the same dominant rhythm.
sensory_bias: proprioceptive awareness — the body's internal sense of position, strain, damage, and capacity
physical weight and effort — characters feel gravity, distance, fatigue
the Ledger's feedback — a character's awareness of their remaining levels as a persistent background sensation
environmental specificity — terrain, weather, architecture rendered through what a body encounters
